behavior: Usually solitary or in pairs; forages slowly and often sits motionless within foliage before gleaning caterpillars and other insects or making short aerial sallies. A long-distance migrant with swift, direct flight and deep, pointed-wing beats.
field_marks: Long, slim body with a very long rounded tail
Grayish warm-brown upperparts and clean whitish underparts
Mostly yellow, slightly downcurved bill with a dark upper ridge
Yellow eye-ring with a subtle dark facial mask
Dark undertail marked with large white spots
habitat: Dense deciduous woodland, forest edges, leafy thickets, second growth, overgrown orchards, and streamside groves, usually where thick cover is broken by gaps or clearings.
